content: We can reframe zero-shot node generalization on a unified graph as an intrinsic curiosity-driven exploration problem, where the ICM curiosity mechanism rewards the model for discovering and encoding unlabeled out-of-distribution nodes that have never been seen during training. This turns the static zero-shot generalization task into an active, self-motivated exploration of the unified graph's novel semantic regions.
source_topics: Zero-shot node generalization on unified graph
trigger_topic: Zero-shot node generalization on unified graph
